title: Loop Spec
description: The open intermediate representation a .loop file parses to. The contract between @loop-lang/parser, @loop-lang/runtime, and the loop-vscode extension. Anyone may implement against this.

config.description: The config tier: which method/preset, when it runs, where. Null when the file has no config block.

use.description: A preset reference: a stdlib name ("BMAD"), a local path ("./my-method.loop"), or a registry ref ("owner/method").

useOverrides.description: Overrides applied to the used preset (the `use X, but: ...` form). Each entry patches a named stage or a top-level config key.

schedule.description: When to run: "manual", "nightly", "on push", or a cron expression. v1 parses but only runs "manual" (via Play).

target.description: Working directory / scope the runner operates in.

definitions.description: Top-level definitions in file order: pipelines and standalone loops.

target.description: What is being overridden: a stage name, or a config key like "schedule".

patch.description: For kind=stage, a partial loop to merge into the stage's loop. For kind=config, a {key,value}.

stages.description: Stages run sequentially in v1; a failing stage halts the rest.

gate.description: A blocking human gate that must be approved before the stage's loop runs (e.g. "a human approves before provisioning").

name.description: Optional name; null for an anonymous loop inside a stage.

doneWhen.description: The predicate the runtime checks to declare the goal met. Null means the loop relies on a human review to stop.

context.description: What the agent may look at before acting (the `look at:` block).

includeLastFailure.description: Whether "and the last failure" was present.

policy.description: Action policy: which action classes run automatically vs require confirmation.

confirm.description: Action classes that pause for human confirmation (e.g. ["migrate","push"]).

cycle.description: The repeated steps, in order. Each step is an agent node executed by the runner.

also.description: Extra finishing passes run in order after the goal is met (e.g. "polish", "run a security check", "update the docs"). Lightweight agent passes; promote to a stage when one needs its own verification.

planSource.description: Where the `plan` step gets its plan. Default (omitted) = the agent generates it. "file" reads the plan from a file (`plan from "<path>"`), so the loop executes a plan you control. The `type` enum is open to future sources (a URL, a command).

path.description: For type=file: the file to read the plan from, relative to the .loop file (or the loop's target dir).

humanPlan.description: "a human approves the plan first" — the plan step is authored/approved by a person before acting.

humanReviewBeforeStop.description: "a human reviews before stopping" — a person judges the output and feeds the verdict back before the loop may stop.

transitions.description: The `when ...:` rules evaluated after each observe.

target.description: For type=test: the test id, e.g. "billing.spec.ts::apostrophe".

command.description: For type=command: the shell command. Default success = exit 0.

expect.description: For type=command: "empty" encodes "finds nothing" (no stdout). Defaults to exit-zero.

description.description: For type=human: what the person should confirm.

on.description: The observed outcome that triggers this rule.

requireGoalMet.description: For on=pass: only fire when doneWhen is also satisfied ("when it passes and the goal is met").

threshold.description: For on=attempts: fire once the attempt count exceeds this ("after 6 tries").

focus.description: For reflect: what to reflect on ("which layer broke").

warn.description: For stop: an optional warning message ("thrashing").

steps.description: Files run in order, fail-fast; each step's text summary carries to the next.

ref.description: File path as written, resolved relative to the flow file.

name.description: Step name = ref basename without extension.

fromStep.description: 'with the result of <name>' — upstream source override.

source.description: Data file to enumerate (.yaml/.yml/.md), relative to the flow file.

forEach.description: Present when this step iterates: run `ref` once per item enumerated from `source`.

modelPolicy.description: Per-phase model tiering. Two tiers (fast/strong) auto-assigned by phase; cascades file→loop→stage.

fast.description: model alias/id for the fast tier

strong.description: model alias/id for the strong tier

isolation.description: How the agent isolates its work. "branch" = a new git branch; "worktree" = a git worktree; "in-place" = no isolation.

branch.description: Branch or worktree name when isolation is "branch" or "worktree".

commit.description: When to commit. "done" = after goal is met; "cycle" = after each cycle; "story" = after each story; "never" = never.

push.description: Whether to push to remote when done.

openPr.description: Whether to open a pull request when done.
